title: Effect of Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG on intestinal permeability in C57BL/6 mice
summary: This study evaluates whether 4-week oral supplementation with L. rhamnosus GG reduces intestinal permeability by at least 30% in C57BL/6 mice compared to controls. Primary outcome is FITC-dextran plasma concentration; secondary outcomes include claudin-1 and occludin expression by Western blot and immunofluorescence.

assumptions: L. rhamnosus GG remains viable during storage and gavage preparation
Mice maintain standard housing conditions throughout study
FITC-dextran 4 kDa is optimal molecular weight for permeability assessment

cited_protocols.title: FITC-dextran intestinal permeability assay in mice
Western blot analysis of tight junction proteins

regulatory_requirements.requirement: IACUC approval for vertebrate animal research
regulatory_requirements.authority: Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee
regulatory_requirements.applicable_because: Study involves live mice with oral gavage procedures
Handling probiotic bacteria and mouse tissues

steps.title: Procure and acclimate mice
steps.description: Obtain 24 male C57BL/6 mice aged 8-10 weeks. House in individually ventilated cages (4 per cage) with 12h light/dark cycle, ad libitum standard chow and water. Acclimate for 7 days prior to treatment initiation.
assumptions: Male mice used to reduce hormonal variability

steps.title: Randomize mice into groups
steps.description: Weigh all mice and randomize into 2 groups (n=12 per group): Control (vehicle) and LGG treatment. Ensure no significant weight difference between groups at baseline.
assumptions: Body weight is primary randomization criterion

steps.title: Prepare probiotic suspension
steps.description: Culture L. rhamnosus GG (ATCC 53103) in MRS broth at 37°C anaerobically for 18h. Harvest by centrifugation (3000g, 10min), wash twice with sterile PBS, resuspend to 1x10^9 CFU/200µL in PBS with 15% glycerol. Aliquot and store at -80°C.
assumptions: Frozen aliquots retain viability for 4 weeks

steps.title: Daily probiotic administration
steps.description: Administer 200µL of LGG suspension (1x10^9 CFU) or PBS vehicle by oral gavage daily for 28 consecutive days. Thaw fresh aliquot weekly. Monitor body weight twice weekly and observe for adverse effects.
assumptions: Daily gavage does not significantly alter gut integrity

steps.title: Monitor animal welfare
steps.description: Record body weight, food intake, and fecal consistency twice weekly. Document any adverse events. Remove any animal with >15% weight loss or signs of distress per IACUC protocol.
assumptions: Probiotic treatment is well-tolerated

steps.title: Verify probiotic colonization
steps.description: At week 2, collect fresh fecal pellets from 4 mice per group. Homogenize in PBS, serially dilute, and plate on MRS agar with vancomycin for Lactobacillus enumeration to confirm gut colonization.
assumptions: Mid-point sampling does not affect outcome measures

steps.title: Fast mice and administer FITC-dextran
steps.description: After 28 days treatment (day 29), fast mice for 4h with water ad libitum. Administer FITC-dextran 4kDa (600mg/kg body weight, 125mg/mL in PBS) by oral gavage. Start timing immediately.
assumptions: 4h fasting reduces gastric content variability

steps.title: Collect blood samples
steps.description: At 4h post-gavage, anesthetize mice with isoflurane and collect 500µL blood via cardiac puncture into EDTA tubes. Centrifuge at 4000g for 15min at 4°C. Collect plasma and store at -20°C protected from light.
assumptions: 4h timepoint captures peak FITC-dextran permeability

steps.title: Measure plasma FITC-dextran concentration
steps.description: Dilute plasma 1:5 in PBS. Load 100µL per well in black 96-well plate in duplicate. Measure fluorescence (excitation 485nm, emission 528nm). Generate standard curve using known FITC-dextran concentrations (0-10µg/mL). Calculate plasma concentration.
assumptions: Plasma auto-fluorescence is negligible after correction

steps.title: Harvest intestinal tissues
steps.description: Immediately after blood collection, euthanize by cervical dislocation. Harvest entire small intestine, flush with ice-cold PBS. Divide into 3 sections: proximal 5cm for protein extraction, mid-section for RNA, distal section for histology in 4% paraformaldehyde. Snap-freeze protein/RNA samples in liquid nitrogen.
assumptions: Ileal tissue shows greatest tight junction changes

steps.title: Prepare tissue lysates for Western blot
steps.description: Homogenize proximal intestine in RIPA buffer (150mM NaCl, 50mM Tris pH7.4, 1% Triton X-100, 0.1% SDS) with protease inhibitors (1:100 Halt cocktail). Incubate 30min on ice, centrifuge 14000g for 15min at 4°C. Collect supernatant, measure protein by BCA assay, aliquot and store at -80°C.
assumptions: RIPA buffer effectively extracts membrane proteins

steps.title: Process tissues for immunofluorescence
steps.description: Fix distal intestine sections in 4% paraformaldehyde overnight at 4°C. Transfer to 30% sucrose for cryoprotection, embed in OCT compound, and cryosection at 7µm thickness. Store slides at -80°C until staining.
assumptions: Frozen sections preserve tight junction epitopes

steps.title: Western blot for tight junction proteins
steps.description: Load 30µg protein per lane on 10% SDS-PAGE gels. Transfer to PVDF membranes, block with 5% milk for 1h. Incubate with primary antibodies: rabbit anti-claudin-1 (1:1000), mouse anti-occludin (1:500), mouse anti-β-actin (1:5000) overnight at 4°C. Wash and incubate with HRP-conjugated secondary antibodies (1:5000) for 1h. Develop with ECL substrate.
assumptions: β-actin is stable housekeeping control

steps.title: Quantify Western blot bands
steps.description: Image blots using ChemiDoc system with multiple exposure times. Analyze band intensity using ImageJ software. Normalize claudin-1 and occludin signals to β-actin. Calculate fold-change relative to control group mean. Analyze n=12 samples per group.
assumptions: Band intensity is linear within detection range

steps.title: Immunofluorescence staining
steps.description: Thaw slides, permeabilize with 0.1% Triton X-100 for 10min, block with 5% BSA for 1h. Incubate with claudin-1 (1:200) or occludin (1:100) antibodies overnight at 4°C. Wash and add Alexa Fluor 488-conjugated secondary antibody (1:500) for 1h. Counterstain with DAPI, mount with ProLong, and image on confocal microscope. Quantify junction continuity on 5 crypts per section.
assumptions: Confocal imaging captures tight junction localization

items.name: RIPA lysis buffer with protease inhibitors

line_items.notes: Per animal cost from Jackson Laboratory
Plates, buffers, ECL substrate, mounting media, etc.

budget.disclaimer: Prices are estimates based on current supplier listings and may vary. Equipment costs excluded assuming existing access.
assumptions: Equipment available through institutional core facilities
Labor costs not included
Animal housing costs estimated at $1 per mouse per day for 35 days included in mouse cost
IACUC approval timeline is 4 weeks
All reagents available within 2 weeks
Core facility equipment accessible with 1 week notice

success_criteria.criterion: Plasma FITC-dextran concentration reduced by at least 30% in LGG group vs control
success_criteria.measurement_method: Fluorescence spectroscopy at 485/528 nm with standard curve

success_criteria.criterion: Claudin-1 protein expression increased in LGG group
success_criteria.measurement_method: Western blot densitometry normalized to β-actin

success_criteria.criterion: Occludin protein expression increased in LGG group
success_criteria.measurement_method: Western blot densitometry normalized to β-actin

controls.name: Vehicle control group
controls.purpose: Establish baseline intestinal permeability and tight junction protein expression without probiotic intervention

controls.name: Beta-actin loading control
controls.purpose: Normalize protein loading across Western blot samples

failure_modes.likely_cause: Inconsistent gavage technique, variable gastric emptying, or plasma hemolysis
failure_modes.mitigation: Standardize fasting time, train single operator for gavage, exclude hemolyzed samples, use technical duplicates

failure_modes.likely_cause: Insufficient probiotic colonization, inadequate dose, or insensitive assay
failure_modes.mitigation: Verify colonization by fecal plating at mid-point, consider dose escalation in follow-up study

failure_modes.likely_cause: Degraded proteins, insufficient tissue, or antibody issues
failure_modes.mitigation: Process tissues immediately on ice, include protease inhibitors, validate antibodies with positive controls
Use experienced technician, house individually if needed, have 10% extra animals

assumptions: Expected 30% reduction in FITC-dextran with SD ~20% based on literature
Two-tailed test with normal distribution
10% attrition rate accounted for
power_calculation.rationale: Sample size of n=12 per group provides 80% power to detect 30% difference with expected variability from published FITC-dextran studies. Total n=24 accounts for potential 10% attrition.

validation.expected_outcome_summary: Successful outcome: LGG-treated mice show ≥30% reduction in plasma FITC-dextran concentration compared to controls, accompanied by significant upregulation of both claudin-1 and occludin proteins in intestinal tissue, demonstrating probiotic-mediated enhancement of intestinal barrier function.
validation.go_no_go_threshold: Plasma FITC-dextran concentration reduced by at least 30% in LGG group with p<0.05
